description_long: This object is a small cluster of wild daisies with three prominent white flowers, each featuring numerous slender petals radiating from a central yellow-orange disc. The flowers are supported by thin, green stems that rise above a dense tuft of narrow, grass-like leaves at the base. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, giving the flowers a classic daisy appearance. The leaves are long, linear, and pointed, contributing to a natural, untamed look typical of wildflowers found in urban parks or roadside verges. The overall structure is airy and delicate, with the flowers spaced apart on individual stems. The coloration is primarily white and green, with subtle yellow-orange centers adding a touch of warmth.
description: A cluster of wild daisies with white petals, yellow-orange centers, and slender green stems rising from a dense base of grass-like leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing three white daisy flowers with yellow-orange centers, all stems and leaves clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled orientation of the flowers and the overlapping of petals, with stems and leaves visible from the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the daisy flowers and the dense arrangement of the grass-like leaves at the base.
description_view_3: Side/rear view with two flowers facing away, showing the green sepals and the spread of the leaves from a different angle.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial pattern around the central disc, with each petal evenly spaced and slightly overlapping at the base, creating a symmetrical, daisy-like whorl.
